text:
Despite running the worst marathon in his largely successful athletics career, Eliud Kipchoge maintains that the Paris Olympics 'was good.'The two-time Olympic champion and the first man to race under two hours in 42km flew the Kenyan flag at the recently concluded Paris Olympics with an eye on a historic third marathon title.Get Full Access for Ksh299/WeekUncover the stories others won’t tell. title: Kipchoge: Paris Olympics was good despite pain
author: Stephen Rutto
description: He says he is back to the drawing board to reflect on opportunities available for him.
